title: King Faisal Specialist Hospital & Research Center to lead global innovation at C3 Davos Silicon Valley Summit
summary: The summit convenes senior executives from healthcare, biotechnology, artificial intelligence, academia, and investment firms under the theme "Healthtech Frontiers: Building the Health Ecosys ...
thbAction: Map the news to THB's provider CRM for referral growth, patient lifecycle engagement, and multi-facility relationship management across hospital and provider networks.
contentHtml:
USA—King Faisal Specialist Hospital & Research Centre (KFSH) will serve as a global strategic partner at the upcoming C3 Davos of Healthcare™ Silicon Valley Summit on April 27, 2026.

The event takes place at the Plug and Play Tech Center in Sunnyvale, California, bringing together industry leaders to shape the future of global healthcare through innovation and collaboration.

Building tomorrow’s health ecosystem

The summit convenes senior executives from healthcare, biotechnology, artificial intelligence, academia, and investment firms under the theme “Healthtech Frontiers: Building the Health Ecosystem of Tomorrow.”

Together, they explore how technology and strategic partnerships can accelerate healthcare transformation and develop next-generation care models that serve patients more effectively.

Dr. Björn Zoëga, Deputy CEO of KFSH, will deliver a keynote address during the summit.

He will discuss how large hospital systems can transition from limited pilot programs to comprehensive digital operating models that span entire health networks.

His presentation will showcase KFSH’s approach to integrating AI-enabled clinical decision support, digital care coordination platforms, and data-driven operational management.

Significantly, these initiatives align with Saudi Arabia’s Vision 2030 Health Sector Transformation Program, demonstrating how institutions can leverage technology to enhance efficiency, clinical outcomes, and patient experiences simultaneously.

AI’s role in healthcare

Dr. Petros Kotsidis, PhD and Chief Digital Officer at KFSH, will participate in the panel discussion “The Future of AI in Healthcare: Consumer-Led or Clinician-Led?”

This session examines a critical question: will patients themselves drive healthcare innovation, or will clinical experts guide technological advancement?

The discussion addresses how this balance shapes technology design, delivery models, and ultimately patient outcomes across the healthcare landscape.

A platform for real-world innovation

The summit attracts more than 300 senior leaders from hospitals, technology companies, and investment groups who focus on translating promising innovations into practical patient care worldwide.

With KFSH’s expanded involvement as a strategic partner, the event emphasizes execution-ready solutions using AI, digital workflows, and advanced therapies that address today’s healthcare challenges.

Recognition of excellence

King Faisal Specialist Hospital ranks first across the Middle East and North Africa and holds the 12th position globally among the world’s top 250 Academic Medical Centers for 2026.

The institution carries multiple distinctions, including recognition as the most valuable healthcare brand in the Kingdom and the Middle East by Brand Finance 2025.

Additionally, Newsweek has recognized KFSH among the World’s Best Hospitals 2025, the World’s Best Smart Hospitals 2026, and the World’s Best Specialized Hospitals 2026, underscoring its commitment to clinical excellence and digital innovation.
